TURNER. HUGUET & BRANS
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
924 MAIN STREET
P. O BOX 1tO
MARTINEZ, CALI FORNIA 94553
TEL (5rO) 22A-3433
FAX {5101 22A-3596
E* /a, (t^

December 11, 19 9I
,fohn wa 1I ace
Attorney at Law
Fair Poli.tical Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
Re: Lease of Mobile Office Site
bv contract Citv Enoineer
Dea r ,John :
I am writing to you on behalf of ANGRISANI, INC., dba
PERMCO, anal Rick Angrisani, its principal owner, who serves as
the Clayton contract city engineer. This letter is to follow
up our telephone conversation of December 10 anal to confirm the
opinion you gave me during that conversation.
The call was promptect by the contract city engineer's
request to lease for a three-year periodl an unimproved site of
some 5,000 square feet in size from the major land developer in
the City. 
The site woulal serve as the location of the
engineer's mobile office structure, the City needing for
expansion the City office space the engineer currently
occupies. The engineer would pay the rent directly to the
deveioper landiord. The rent will equate to fair market rentai
and was arrived at by the parties to approximate a I0% return
on the estimated fair market value of the site in its current
undeveloped condition. 
Based on the foregoing facts, it was
your opinion that the city engineer would not have placed
itself in a conflict position of being unable to act upon
projects of the developer in the engineer's officiaL position.
The engineer, of course, will abstain from any official
activity concerning the actual 6,000 square foot site.
You pointed out that if the lease were at a discounted
value below its fair market rental, or if the developer loaned
or advanced funds to the engineer in connection with placement of


December 11", 19 91
Page 2
the modular unit on the site, then those events could
constitute gifts disqualifying the engineer. Those
circumstances will not exist in our situation.
I would appreciate it if at your convenience you could give
me a brief response confirming your advice as above stateal .
I appreciate your assistance in this matter and as usual I
find the help from your office prompt anil professional.
Very truly yours,
Maurice E. Huguet,
MEH : vb
29 27}]
cc: City Eng ineer
City Manager
Mayor Manning

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a contract city engineer who leases a mobile office site from a major land developer at fair market value creates a conflict of interest that would disqualify the engineer from acting on the developer's projects.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter (not an FPPC response/advice letter). The document references a prior telephone conversation with FPPC staff where oral advice was given, and the sender is requesting written confirmation of that advice. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The letter describes the factual scenario and the oral opinion received, but does not include FPPC's written response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether a contract city engineer who leases a mobile office site from a major land developer at fair market value creates a conflict of interest that would disqualify the engineer from acting on the developer's projects.

embedding.summary: An attorney writes to FPPC staff requesting written confirmation of prior telephone advice regarding whether a contract city engineer's lease of an office site from a major land developer at fair market value creates a disqualifying conflict of interest.
